I need to understand the difference between nameplate regime rendering and vessel regime rendering in this project. 

Find and read these files thoroughly:
1. The nameplate regime code — look for `rbrn_regime.sh` or similar in `Tools/rbk/`
2. The vessel regime code — look for `rbrv_regime.sh` or similar in `Tools/rbk/`
3. The workbench dispatch for colophons `rbw-rnr`, `rbw-rnv`, `rbw-rvr`, `rbw-rvv` — likely in `Tools/rbk/rbw_workbench.sh`

For each regime file, I need to see:
- The render function (what gets printed when you run the render tabtarget)
- The validate function (what gets printed when you run the validate tabtarget)
- How they source/display the env file contents
- Any formatting, grouping, or teaching text

Report the full code of the render and validate functions for BOTH nameplate and vessel regimes. Include line numbers.

43	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_RUNTIME                              "Container runtime: docker or podman" \

48	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Bottle Image"
49	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Sentry Image"
50	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Bottle Image"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Entry Service Configuration"
53	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE                           "Entry functionality: disabled or enabled" \
54	                     disabled enabled

56	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION               "External port on Transit Network"
57	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE                   "Enclave port between Sentry and Bottle"
58

60	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP                      "Base IPv4 for enclave network"

65	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Core"
66	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN                      "Minimum port for outbound connections"
67	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE                      "DNS mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
68	                     disabled global allowlist

93	# Enforce all RBRN enrollment validations and custom format checks
94	zrbrn_enforce() {
95	  zrbrn_sentinel
96	
97	  buv_vet RBRN
98	
99	  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
100	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
101	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
102	
103	  # Cross-port check (entry ports must be less than uplink port min)
104	  if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "enabled"; then
105	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
106	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
107	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
108	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
109	  fi
110	
111	  # Build docker env args array from validated values
112	  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
113	  buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
114	}

116	######################################################################
117	# Public Functions (rbrn_*)
118	
119	# List available nameplate monikers as space-separated tokens
120	# Prerequisite: RBCC sourced (needs RBBC_dot_dir, RBCC_rbrn_file)
121	rbrn_list_capture() {
122	  zrbcc_sentinel
123	
124	  local z_result=""
125	  local z_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
126	  local z_i=""
127	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
128	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
129	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
130	    local z_moniker="${z_dir##*/}"
131	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_moniker}"
132	  done
133	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
134	  echo "${z_result}"
135	}
136	
137	######################################################################
138	# Cross-Nameplate Functions
139	#
140	# rbrn_preflight:  Requires RBCC kindled (called from CLI rbrn_audit)
141	
142	# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
143	zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
144	  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
145	  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
146	  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
147	}
148	
149	# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
150	# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
151	zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
152	  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
153	  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
154	  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
155	  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
156	  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then
157	    buc_die "${z_label}=${z_ip} is not within subnet ${z_base}/${z_mask}"
158	  fi
159	}
160	
161	# Cross-nameplate conflict validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
162	# Checks: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, enclave IP uniqueness
163	rbrn_preflight() {
164	  zrbcc_sentinel
165	
166	  # Collect structured data from all nameplates via isolation subshells
167	  local z_nameplate_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
168	  local z_data_lines=()
169	  local z_nf_i=""
170	  for z_nf_i in "${!z_nameplate_files[@]}"; do
171	    test -f "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}" || continue
172	    local z_line
173	    z_line=$(
174	      bash -c '
175	        source "$1" || exit 1
176	        echo "${RBRN_MONIKER}|${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-0}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-0}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
177	      ' _ "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
178	    ) || buc_die "Preflight isolation failed for: ${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
179	    z_data_lines+=("${z_line}")
180	  done
181	
182	  # Parallel arrays for conflict detection (bash 3.2 compatible)
183	  local z_ws_port_keys=()
184	  local z_ws_port_vals=()
185	  local z_enc_port_keys=()
186	  local z_enc_port_vals=()
187	  local z_ip_keys=()
188	  local z_ip_vals=()
189	  local z_net_starts=()
190	  local z_net_ends=()
191	  local z_net_owners=()
192	
193	  local z_mon=""
194	  local z_entry=""
195	  local z_ws=""
196	  local z_enc=""
197	  local z_base=""
198	  local z_mask=""
199	  local z_sentry=""
200	  local z_bottle=""
201	  for z_nf_i in "${!z_data_lines[@]}"; do
202	    IFS='|' read -r z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle <<< "${z_data_lines[$z_nf_i]}" \
203	      || buc_die "Failed to parse nameplate data line"
204	    test -n "${z_mon}" || continue
205	
206	    # Workstation and enclave port uniqueness (enabled entries only)
207	    if test "${z_entry}" = "enabled"; then
208	      local z_i
209	      for z_i in "${!z_ws_port_keys[@]}"; do
210	        if test "${z_ws_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_ws}"; then
211	          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${z_ws} claimed by both ${z_ws_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
212	        fi
213	      done
214	      z_ws_port_keys+=("${z_ws}")
215	      z_ws_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
216	
217	      for z_i in "${!z_enc_port_keys[@]}"; do
218	        if test "${z_enc_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_enc}"; then
219	          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${z_enc} claimed by both ${z_enc_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
220	        fi
221	      done
222	      z_enc_port_keys+=("${z_enc}")
223	      z_enc_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
224	    fi
225	
226	    # Enclave IP uniqueness (all sentry and bottle IPs across nameplates)
227	    local z_j
228	    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
229	      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_sentry}"; then
230	        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_sentry} claimed by ${z_mon} (sentry) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
231	      fi
232	    done
233	    z_ip_keys+=("${z_sentry}")
234	    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:sentry")
235	
236	    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
237	      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_bottle}"; then
238	        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_bottle} claimed by ${z_mon} (bottle) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
239	      fi
240	    done
241	    z_ip_keys+=("${z_bottle}")
242	    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:bottle")
243	
244	    # Subnet non-overlap
245	    local z_net_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
246	    local z_net_mask_bits=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
247	    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
248	    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))
249	    local z_net_end=$(( z_net_addr + z_net_size - 1 ))
250	
251	    local z_k
252	    for z_k in "${!z_net_starts[@]}"; do
253	      if [[ ${z_net_addr} -le ${z_net_ends[$z_k]} ]] && [[ ${z_net_starts[$z_k]} -le ${z_net_end} ]]; then
254	        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
255	      fi
256	    done
257	    z_net_starts+=("${z_net_addr}")
258	    z_net_ends+=("${z_net_end}")
259	    z_net_owners+=("${z_mon}")
260	
261	  done
262	}

20	
21	# Multiple inclusion guard
22	test -z "${ZBUV_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
23	ZBUV_INCLUDED=1

25	# Literal constants — check capture output protocol markers
26	BUV_check_gated="gated"

30	ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
31	source "${ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
32	
33	buv_file_exists() {
34	  local z_filepath="${1:-}"
35	  test -f "${z_filepath}" || buc_die "Required file not found: ${z_filepath}"
36	}
37	
38	buv_dir_exists() {
39	  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
40	  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
41	}
42	
43	buv_dir_empty() {
44	  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
45	  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
46	  local z_check_file
47	  z_check_file=$(mktemp)
48	  find "${z_dirpath}" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -print -quit > "${z_check_file}"
49	  test ! -s "${z_check_file}" || { rm -f "${z_check_file}"; buc_die "Directory must be empty: ${z_dirpath}"; }
50	  rm -f "${z_check_file}"
51	}
52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Enrollment infrastructure
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	zbuv_kindle() {
58	  test -z "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buv already kindled"
59	
60	  # Enrollment rolls (9 parallel arrays)
61	  z_buv_scope_roll=()
62	  z_buv_varname_roll=()
63	  z_buv_type_roll=()
64	  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
65	  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
66	  z_buv_p1_roll=()
67	  z_buv_p2_roll=()
68	  z_buv_group_roll=()
69	  z_buv_desc_roll=()
70	
71	  # Group registry rolls (4 parallel arrays, foreign-keyed by group title)
72	  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
73	  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
74	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
75	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()
76	
77	  # Mutable kindle state: regime context (set by buv_regime_enroll / buv_group_enroll / buv_gate_enroll)
78	  z_buv_current_scope=""
79	  z_buv_current_group=""
80	  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
81	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
82	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
83	
84	  readonly ZBUV_KINDLED=1
85	}
86	
87	zbuv_sentinel() {
88	  test "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buv not kindled - call zbuv_kindle first"
89	}
90	
91	# Test support: reset enrollment state without re-kindling
92	zbuv_reset_enrollment() {
93	  zbuv_sentinel
94	
95	  # Clear enrollment rolls
96	  z_buv_scope_roll=()
97	  z_buv_varname_roll=()
98	  z_buv_type_roll=()
99	  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
100	  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
101	  z_buv_p1_roll=()
102	  z_buv_p2_roll=()
103	  z_buv_group_roll=()
104	  z_buv_desc_roll=()
105	
106	  # Clear group registry rolls
107	  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
108	  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
109	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
110	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()
111	
112	  # Reset regime context state
113	  z_buv_current_scope=""
114	  z_buv_current_group=""
115	  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
116	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
117	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
118	}
119	
120	# Regime context setters — called during kindle to establish enrollment scope
121	
122	# buv_regime_enroll SCOPE — set current enrollment scope
123	# Validates that SCOPE is non-empty. All subsequent enroll calls use this scope
124	# until another buv_regime_enroll is called.
125	buv_regime_enroll() {
126	  zbuv_sentinel
127	
128	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
129	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_regime_enroll: scope required"
130	  z_buv_current_scope="${z_scope}"
131	  z_buv_current_group=""
132	  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
133	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
134	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
135	}
136	
137	# buv_group_enroll TITLE — set current group context
138	# Creates a group registry entry. All subsequent enroll calls are tagged with
139	# this group title until the next buv_group_enroll call.
140	# Resets gate context — use buv_gate_enroll after this to gate items.
141	buv_group_enroll() {
142	  zbuv_sentinel
143	
144	  test -n "${z_buv_current_scope}" || buc_die "buv_group_enroll: call buv_regime_enroll first"
145	
146	  local z_title="${1:-}"
147	  test -n "${z_title}" || buc_die "buv_group_enroll: title required"
148	
149	  z_buv_grp_scope_roll+=("${z_buv_current_scope}")
150	  z_buv_grp_title_roll+=("${z_title}")
151	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll+=("")
152	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll+=("")
153	
154	  z_buv_current_group="${z_title}"
155	  z_buv_current_group_idx=$(( ${#z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]} - 1 ))
156	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
157	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
158	}
159	
160	# buv_gate_enroll GATE_VAR GATE_VAL — set gate context within current group
161	# All subsequent variable enrolls in this group are gated by this condition.
162	# Also registers the gate on the current group for render section headers.
163	buv_gate_enroll() {
164	  zbuv_sentinel
165	
166	  test -n "${z_buv_current_group}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: call buv_group_enroll first"
167	
168	  local z_gate_var="${1:-}"
169	  local z_gate_val="${2:-}"
170	  test -n "${z_gate_var}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: gate variable required"
171	  test -n "${z_gate_val}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: gate value required"
172	
173	  z_buv_current_gate_var="${z_gate_var}"
174	  z_buv_current_gate_val="${z_gate_val}"
175	
176	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[z_buv_current_group_idx]="${z_gate_var}"
177	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[z_buv_current_group_idx]="${z_gate_val}"
178	}
179	
180	# Internal enrollment helper — all public enroll functions delegate here
181	# Usage: zbuv_enroll VARNAME TYPE P1 P2 DESC
182	# Gate context inherited from buv_gate_enroll; group from buv_group_enroll.
183	zbuv_enroll() {
184	  zbuv_sentinel
185	
186	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
187	  local z_type="${2:-}"
188	  local z_p1="${3:-}"
189	  local z_p2="${4:-}"
190	  local z_desc="${5:-}"
191	
192	  test -n "${z_buv_current_scope}" || buc_die "zbuv_enroll: call buv_regime_enroll first"
193	  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] || buc_die "zbuv_enroll: invalid variable name: '${z_varname}'"
194	
195	  z_buv_scope_roll+=("${z_buv_current_scope}")
196	  z_buv_varname_roll+=("${z_varname}")
197	  z_buv_type_roll+=("${z_type}")
198	  z_buv_gate_var_roll+=("${z_buv_current_gate_var}")
199	  z_buv_gate_val_roll+=("${z_buv_current_gate_val}")
200	  z_buv_p1_roll+=("${z_p1}")

15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUK Presentation Regime - Shared rendering utilities for regime CLI modules
20	#
21	# Provides section-based rendering with gate-aware suppression and
22	# terminal-adaptive layouts.  Each regime CLI (rbrn_cli, rbrv_cli)
23	# sources this module and calls the public functions to compose its
24	# own render output.  Formatting mechanics are shared; editorial
25	# decisions (section ordering, grouping) stay manual per regime CLI.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Multiple inclusion detection
30	test -z "${ZBUPR_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bupr multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
31	ZBUPR_SOURCED=1
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Functions (zbupr_*)
35	
36	zbupr_kindle() {
37	  test -z "${ZBUPR_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bupr already kindled"
38	
39	  # Terminal layout from BURD dispatch (set in bul_launcher before pipe)
40	  readonly ZBUPR_TERM_COLS=${BURD_TERM_COLS:-80}
41	  if test "${ZBUPR_TERM_COLS}" -ge 120; then
42	    readonly ZBUPR_LAYOUT=single
43	  else
44	    readonly ZBUPR_LAYOUT=double
45	  fi
46	
47	
48	  # Mutable kindle state: section render tracking
49	  z_bupr_section_active=1
50	  z_bupr_section_gate_desc=""
51	  z_bupr_section_suppressed=()
52	
53	  readonly ZBUPR_KINDLED=1
54	}
55	
56	zbupr_sentinel() {
57	  test "${ZBUPR_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bupr not kindled - call zbupr_kindle first"
58	}
59	
60	######################################################################
61	# Public Functions (bupr_*)
62	
63	# bupr_section_begin TITLE [GATE_VAR GATE_VALUE]
64	#
65	# Begin a render section.  Prints section header.
66	# Optional gate: evaluates ${!GATE_VAR} against GATE_VALUE.
67	# If gate not satisfied, prints collapsed reminder and suppresses
68	# subsequent bupr_section_item calls until bupr_section_end.
69	bupr_section_begin() {
70	  zbupr_sentinel
71	  local z_title="$1"
72	  local z_gate_var=${2:-}
73	  local z_gate_value=${3:-}
74	
75	  z_bupr_section_suppressed=()
76	  z_bupr_section_gate_desc=""
77	
78	  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
79	    local z_actual=${!z_gate_var:-}
80	    if test "${z_actual}" = "${z_gate_value}"; then
81	      z_bupr_section_active=1
82	    else
83	      z_bupr_section_active=0
84	      z_bupr_section_gate_desc="${z_gate_var}=${z_actual}"
85	    fi
86	  else
87	    z_bupr_section_active=1
88	  fi
89	
90	  if test "${z_bupr_section_active}" = 1; then
91	    if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
92	      printf "${BUC_yellow}%-34s${BUC_reset} ${BUC_green}(since %s=%s)${BUC_reset}\n" \
93	        "${z_title}" "${z_gate_var}" "${z_gate_value}"
94	    else
95	      printf "${BUC_yellow}%s${BUC_reset}\n" "${z_title}"
96	    fi
97	  else
98	    printf "${BUC_yellow}%-34s${BUC_reset} ${BUC_green}(%s)${BUC_reset}\n" "${z_title}" "${z_bupr_section_gate_desc}"
99	  fi
100	}
101	
102	# bupr_section_end
103	#
104	# End a render section.  Resets section state for next section.
105	bupr_section_end() {
106	  zbupr_sentinel
107	  z_bupr_section_active=1
108	  z_bupr_section_gate_desc=""
109	  z_bupr_section_suppressed=()
110	}
111	
112	# bupr_item VARNAME TYPE REQ_STATUS DESCRIPTION
113	#
114	# Render one regime field outside any section.
115	# Same layout as bupr_section_item but ignores section state.
116	bupr_item() {
117	  zbupr_sentinel
118	  zbupr_render_field "$@"
119	}
120	
121	# bupr_section_item VARNAME TYPE REQ_STATUS DESCRIPTION
122	#
123	# Render one regime field within a section.
124	#   VARNAME:     unquoted regime variable name (e.g., RBRN_ENTRY_MODE)
125	#   TYPE:        unquoted type badge (xname, string, fqin, port, ipv4, etc.)
126	#   REQ_STATUS:  unquoted — req, opt, or cond
127	#   DESCRIPTION: quoted human prose
128	#
129	# If section is collapsed (gate not satisfied), appends VARNAME to
130	# suppressed list and returns silently.
131	bupr_section_item() {
132	  zbupr_sentinel
133	
134	  # Collapsed section — track and skip
135	  if test "${z_bupr_section_active}" = 0; then
136	    z_bupr_section_suppressed+=("$1")
137	    return 0
138	  fi
139	
140	  zbupr_render_field "$@"
141	}
142	
143	# zbupr_render_field VARNAME TYPE REQ_STATUS DESCRIPTION
144	#
145	# Shared rendering logic for bupr_item and bupr_section_item.
146	# Reads ZBUPR_LAYOUT to choose single-line or double-line format.
147	zbupr_render_field() {
148	  local z_varname=$1
149	  local z_type=$2
150	  local z_req=$3
151	  local z_desc="$4"
152	  local z_value=${!z_varname:-}
153	
154	  # Secret redaction — replace value before any rendering
155	  if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_value}"; then
156	    z_value="(redacted — ${#z_value} chars)"
157	  fi
158	
159	  # Name color: green when set, yellow when not set
160	  local z_nc
161	  if test -n "${z_value}"; then
162	    z_nc=${BUC_green}
163	  else
164	    z_nc=${BUC_yellow}
165	    z_value="(not set)"
166	  fi
167	
168	  if test "${ZBUPR_LAYOUT}" = single; then
169	    # Wide terminal: name value req type description — one line
170	    printf "  ${z_nc}%-30s${BUC_reset}  %-24s  ${BUC_blue}%-4s %-11s${BUC_reset}  ${BUC_cyan}%s${BUC_reset}\n" \
171	      "${z_varname}" "${z_value}" "${z_req}" "${z_type}" "${z_desc}"
172	  else
173	    # Narrow terminal: 3-line short display
174	    # Line 1: name + req + type
175	    printf "  ${z_nc}%-30s${BUC_reset}  ${BUC_blue}%-4s %-11s${BUC_reset}\n" \
176	      "${z_varname}" "${z_req}" "${z_type}"
177	    # Line 2: current value
178	    printf "      ${BUC_white}%s${BUC_reset}\n" "${z_value}"
179	    # Line 3: meaning
180	    printf "      ${BUC_gray}meaning => %s${BUC_reset}\n" "${z_desc}"
181	  fi
182	}
183	
184	# eof

712	buv_report() {
713	  zbuv_sentinel
714	
715	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
716	  local z_label="${2:-}"
717	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_report: scope required"
718	  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_report: label required"
719	
720	  local z_any_failed=0
721	
722	  buc_step "${z_label}"
723	
724	  local z_i
725	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
726	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
727	
728	    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
729	    local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
730	    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
731	    local z_err
732	
733	    # Secret redaction — replace display value before PASS/FAIL output
734	    local z_display_val="${z_val}"
735	    if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_val}"; then
736	      z_display_val="(redacted — ${#z_val} chars)"
737	    fi
738	
739	    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
740	    if test -z "${z_err}"; then
741	      buc_step "  PASS  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]"
742	    elif test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}"; then
743	      buc_step "  SKIP  ${z_varname} (gated)"
744	    else
745	      buc_step "  FAIL  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
746	      z_any_failed=1
747	    fi
748	  done
749	
750	  return "${z_any_failed}"
751	}
752	
753	# zbuv_group_gate_recite SCOPE TITLE — look up group gate from registry
754	# Sets ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR and ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL (empty if ungated).
755	zbuv_group_gate_recite() {
756	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
757	  local z_title="${2:-}"
758	
759	  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR=""
760	  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL=""
761	
762	  local z_s
763	  for z_s in "${!z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]}"; do
764	    if test "${z_buv_grp_scope_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_scope}" \
765	      && test "${z_buv_grp_title_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_title}"; then
766	      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR="${z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[$z_s]}"
767	      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL="${z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[$z_s]}"
768	      return 0
769	    fi
770	  done
771	}
772	
773	# zbuv_req_status INDEX — derive req/opt/cond from enrollment data
774	# Sets ZBUV_REQ_STATUS.
775	zbuv_req_status() {
776	  local z_idx="${1:-}"
777	  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
778	  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"
779	
780	  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
781	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="cond"
782	  elif test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then
783	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="opt"
784	  else
785	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="req"
786	  fi
787	}
788	
789	# buv_render SCOPE "Label" — render all enrolled vars via bupr_ presentation
790	# Walks enrollment rolls grouped by group, applying group-level gates.
791	# Requires bupr (PresentationRegime) to be kindled.
792	buv_render() {
793	  zbuv_sentinel
794	  zbupr_sentinel
795	
796	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
797	  local z_label="${2:-}"
798	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_render: scope required"
799	  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_render: label required"
800	
801	  local z_current_group=""
802	  local z_i
803	  local z_group=""
804	  local z_varname=""
805	  local z_type=""
806	  local z_desc=""
807	
808	  echo ""
809	  echo "${BUC_white}${z_label}${BUC_reset}"
810	  echo ""
811	
812	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
813	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
814	
815	    z_group="${z_buv_group_roll[$z_i]}"
816	    z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
817	    z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
818	    z_desc="${z_buv_desc_roll[$z_i]}"
819	
820	    # Group transition — close previous, open new
821	    if test "${z_group}" != "${z_current_group}"; then
822	      if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
823	        bupr_section_end
824	      fi
825	      z_current_group="${z_group}"
826	
827	      zbuv_group_gate_recite "${z_scope}" "${z_group}"
828	      if test -n "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}"; then
829	        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL}"
830	      else
831	        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}"
832	      fi
833	    fi
834	
835	    zbuv_req_status "${z_i}"
836	    bupr_section_item "${z_varname}" "${z_type}" "${ZBUV_REQ_STATUS}" "${z_desc}"
837	  done
838	
839	  # Close final group
840	  if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
841	    bupr_section_end
842	  fi
843	}

```bash
buv_report() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_label="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_report: scope required"
  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_report: label required"

  local z_any_failed=0

  buc_step "${z_label}"

  local z_i
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue

    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
    local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
    local z_err

    # Secret redaction — replace display value before PASS/FAIL output
    local z_display_val="${z_val}"
    if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_val}"; then
      z_display_val="(redacted — ${#z_val} chars)"
    fi

    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
    if test -z "${z_err}"; then
      buc_step "  PASS  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]"
    elif test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}"; then
      buc_step "  SKIP  ${z_varname} (gated)"
    else
      buc_step "  FAIL  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
      z_any_failed=1
    fi
  done

  return "${z_any_failed}"
}
```

### RENDER Function (buv_render)

buv_render() {
  zbuv_sentinel
  zbupr_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_label="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_render: scope required"
  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_render: label required"

  local z_current_group=""
  local z_i
  local z_group=""
  local z_varname=""
  local z_type=""
  local z_desc=""

  echo ""
  echo "${BUC_white}${z_label}${BUC_reset}"
  echo ""

  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue

    z_group="${z_buv_group_roll[$z_i]}"
    z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
    z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
    z_desc="${z_buv_desc_roll[$z_i]}"

    # Group transition — close previous, open new
    if test "${z_group}" != "${z_current_group}"; then
      if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
        bupr_section_end
      fi
      z_current_group="${z_group}"

      zbuv_group_gate_recite "${z_scope}" "${z_group}"
      if test -n "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}"; then
        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL}"
      else
        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}"
      fi
    fi

    zbuv_req_status "${z_i}"
    bupr_section_item "${z_varname}" "${z_type}" "${ZBUV_REQ_STATUS}" "${z_desc}"
  done

  # Close final group
  if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
    bupr_section_end
  fi
}
```

zbupr_render_field() {
  local z_varname=$1
  local z_type=$2
  local z_req=$3
  local z_desc="$4"
  local z_value=${!z_varname:-}

  # Secret redaction — replace value before any rendering
  if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_value}"; then
    z_value="(redacted — ${#z_value} chars)"
  fi

  # Name color: green when set, yellow when not set
  local z_nc
  if test -n "${z_value}"; then
    z_nc=${BUC_green}
  else
    z_nc=${BUC_yellow}
    z_value="(not set)"
  fi

  if test "${ZBUPR_LAYOUT}" = single; then
    # Wide terminal: name value req type description — one line
    printf "  ${z_nc}%-30s${BUC_reset}  %-24s  ${BUC_blue}%-4s %-11s${BUC_reset}  ${BUC_cyan}%s${BUC_reset}\n" \
      "${z_varname}" "${z_value}" "${z_req}" "${z_type}" "${z_desc}"
  else
    # Narrow terminal: 3-line short display
    # Line 1: name + req + type
    printf "  ${z_nc}%-30s${BUC_reset}  ${BUC_blue}%-4s %-11s${BUC_reset}\n" \
      "${z_varname}" "${z_req}" "${z_type}"
    # Line 2: current value
    printf "      ${BUC_white}%s${BUC_reset}\n" "${z_value}"
    # Line 3: meaning
    printf "      ${BUC_gray}meaning => %s${BUC_reset}\n" "${z_desc}"
  fi
}
```

### KEY DIFFERENCE: Enrollment Structure

The **only substantive difference** between nameplate and vessel rendering is in the **enrollment definitions**, not the rendering functions themselves:

**Nameplate Enrollment** (`rbrn_regime.sh`, lines 30-87):
- Groups: "Core Service Identity", "Container Image Configuration", "Entry Service Configuration", "Enclave Network Configuration", "Uplink Core", "Uplink DNS Allowlist", "Uplink Access Allowlist"
- Scope: `RBRN`
- ~40 enrolled variables managing container runtime, networking, entry ports, enclave IPs, etc.

**Vessel Enrollment** (`rbrv_regime.sh`, lines 30-85):
- Groups: "Core Vessel Identity", "Tool Image Reliquary", "Egress Mode", "Binding Configuration", "Conjuring Configuration", "Image Group", "Grafting Configuration"
- Scope: `RBRV`
- ~20 enrolled variables managing vessel mode (bind/conjure/graft), image sources, build context, etc.

Both regimes use the **same `buv_report()` and `buv_render()` functions**, which iterate through their respective enrollment rolls and apply the same formatting, grouping, and gating logic. The render output is grouped by section headers with gate-aware suppression (e.g., binding configuration fields only appear when `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=bind`).
